query: I'm designing a grad-level applied stats course and need a criterion-referenced rubric for the final project — a 10-page applied analysis on a dataset of the student's choosing. The course learning objectives are: (a) frame a research question, (b) choose appropriate statistical methods, (c) check assumptions, (d) report effect sizes with uncertainty, (e) document reproducibly. Help me author the rubric BEFORE I see any submissions so I'm not tempted to drift the criteria.
expected_behavior: Produces a criterion-referenced rubric with 4-6 named criteria (not 8+, not 2), each mapped to at least one of the named learning objectives, with no learning objective left uncovered.

Uses 3 or 4 or 5 proficiency bands per criterion where each band descriptor names OBSERVABLE evidence a grader could check in the submitted work (e.g., 'assumption checks performed and acted on') rather than vague descriptors like 'good methodology' or 'excellent analysis'.
Recommends or explicitly notes the pre-grading inter-grader calibration step (instructor + TA apply the rubric to a sample work together before any real grading begins) as part of deployment.
